chief_complaint: A 60-year-old man presented with vertigo, vomiting, slurred speech, and hearing loss on one side.
history: The patient's condition quickly worsened, resulting in left-sided hemiplegia, dysphagia, and ataxia. He had a history of vertigo and vomiting. He was on anticoagulant treatment.
exam: Vital signs: not mentioned. Physical examination findings: left-sided hemiplegia, dysphagia, and ataxia.
labs: Imaging revealed a basilar artery dissecting aneurysm (BADA) with a mural thrombus, compressing the brainstem. Follow-up imaging showed new strokes.
correct_diagnosis: Basilar artery dissecting aneurysm with ischemic stroke
differentials: Basilar artery dissecting aneurysm with ischemic stroke
correct_ranking: Basilar artery dissecting aneurysm with ischemic stroke
